text:
Title: General Pesticide Safety Guidelines
Category: Safety
Before use:
- Read the entire product label and SDS.
- Confirm crop, pest, and registration status in your country.
- Calibrate sprayer and use correct PPE.
During use:
- Do not eat, drink, or smoke while handling pesticides.
- Avoid spraying in strong wind or near bee activity.
- Never guess dosage; use only label-approved rates.
If information is missing:
- Do not invent mixing instructions or dosage.
- Consult a licensed agricultural
title: General Pesticide Safety Guidelines

text:
- Do not invent mixing instructions or dosage.
- Consult a licensed agricultural advisor or the product manufacturer.
- Check the physical product label on the container.
After use:
- Triple-rinse containers and dispose according to local regulations.
- Wash PPE separately from household laundry.
title: General Pesticide Safety Guidelines

text:
Product: AgroShield 720 SC (example)
Active ingredient: Chlorothalonil 720 g/L
Type: Fungicide
Registered crops: Tomato, potato, cucurbits (check local label)
Target: Early blight, late blight, anthracnose (label-dependent)
Application (example label guidance):
- Tomato early blight: 1.5 L per hectare in 200-400 L water.
- Interval: 7-10 days; maximum 4 applications per season unless label states otherwise.
Safety:
- Harmful if swallowed. Avoid drift to water bodies.
- REI (re-entry): follow lab
title: AgroShield 720 SC (example)

text:
Harmful if swallowed. Avoid drift to water bodies.
- REI (re-entry): follow label (often 12-24 hours).
- Store locked away from food and feed.
Note: Always use the official product label for your country and registration.
title: AgroShield 720 SC (example)

text:
Product: WheatGuard Pro 250 EC (example)
Active ingredient: Propiconazole 250 g/L
Type: Fungicide (triazole)
Registered crops: Wheat, barley (check local label)
Target: Yellow rust, brown rust, powdery mildew (label-dependent)
Application (example label guidance):
- Wheat yellow rust: 0.5 L per hectare at first sign of disease.
- Do not apply within 35 days of harvest unless label allows.
Safety:
- Wear chemical-resistant gloves and mask during mixing and spraying.
- Do not tank-mix with product
title: WheatGuard Pro 250 EC (example)

text:
stant gloves and mask during mixing and spraying.
- Do not tank-mix with products unless label permits.
- Toxic to aquatic organisms; buffer zones required near water.
Note: Dosage and intervals must match the registered label for your region.
title: WheatGuard Pro 250 EC (example)

text:
Title: Tomato Early Blight (Alternaria solani)
Crop: Tomato
Category: Disease management
Symptoms:
Brown concentric rings on older leaves, yellowing around spots, leaf drop in severe cases.
Treatment:
- Apply a fungicide containing chlorothalonil every 7-10 days when conditions are humid.
- Remove and destroy infected leaves; do not compost them.
- Avoid overhead irrigation; water at the base of plants.
- Improve spacing for air circulation.
Safety:
Always read the product label before applicati
title: Tomato Early Blight (Alternaria solani)

text:
cing for air circulation.
Safety:
Always read the product label before application. Wear gloves and eye protection.
title: Tomato Early Blight (Alternaria solani)

text:
Title: Wheat Yellow Rust (Puccinia striiformis)
Crop: Wheat
Category: Disease management
Symptoms:
Bright yellow stripes on leaves, reduced photosynthesis, yield loss if untreated early.
Treatment:
- Spray propiconazole or tebuconazole at early infection stages.
- Use locally approved resistant varieties where available.
- Monitor fields weekly during cool, wet spring weather.
Safety:
Follow label rates and pre-harvest intervals. Do not exceed maximum applications per season.
title: Wheat Yellow Rust (Puccinia striiformis)
